name: threejs-skills
description: Create 3D scenes, interactive experiences, and visual effects using Three.js. Use when user requests 3D graphics, WebGL experiences, 3D visualizations, animations, or interactive 3D elements.
capabilities: Generate complete Three.js scene setup with camera, renderer, and lighting
Create interactive 3D objects with mouse controls and raycasting
Implement particle systems and visual effects
Build responsive 3D experiences with window resize handling
Apply materials, textures, and shadows for realistic rendering
Provide version-compatible code for Three.js r128 CDN
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains documentation-only content (SKILL.md) teaching Three.js 3D graphics development. All 82 static analysis findings are false positives: backtick characters are Markdown code block delimiters, not shell execution; URLs are CDN resource references for the Three.js library; cryptographic warnings are pattern mismatches on version numbers and documentation text. No executable code, no network calls, no command injection vectors exist in this educational content.
valueStatement: Building 3D web experiences requires understanding scene setup, lighting, and rendering patterns. This skill provides systematic Three.js patterns for creating performant interactive 3D content.
userTitle: Create 3D graphics and interactive scenes with Three.js
limitations: Uses Three.js r128 CDN which lacks newer features like CapsuleGeometry
Cannot import OrbitControls or advanced addons from CDN
Advanced post-processing effects require production build tools
Does not support GLTF model loading without build environment

useCases.title: Interactive Product Visualization
useCases.description: Create 3D product displays that users can rotate, zoom, and explore directly in their browser.

useCases.targetUser: Data analysts and business intelligence professionals
useCases.title: Data Visualization Dashboard
useCases.description: Transform complex datasets into immersive 3D charts, graphs, and spatial representations.

useCases.title: Educational 3D Content
useCases.description: Build interactive models for teaching anatomy, astronomy, architecture, or engineering concepts.

promptTemplates.title: Basic 3D Scene Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a basic Three.js scene with a rotating cube. Include scene setup, camera, renderer with antialiasing, a colored cube geometry, lighting, and an animation loop. Add window resize handling.

promptTemplates.title: Interactive Sphere with Mouse Controls
promptTemplates.prompt: Build an interactive 3D sphere that responds to mouse movement. Implement custom camera controls (drag to rotate, scroll to zoom) without OrbitControls. Use MeshStandardMaterial with proper lighting.

promptTemplates.title: Particle System Animation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a particle system with 1000+ particles using BufferGeometry. Add animation so particles move or rotate over time. Include proper performance practices like geometry and material disposal.
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding visual effects to web experiences

promptTemplates.title: Raycasting Object Selection
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement raycasting to detect mouse clicks and hovers on 3D objects. When hovering, scale the object and change cursor style. When clicking, change the object color. Include an array of clickable objects.

bestPractices: Always use Three.js r128 CDN for artifact compatibility and verify feature availability
Add window resize handlers to update camera aspect ratio and renderer dimensions
Dispose of geometries, materials, and textures when removing objects to prevent memory leaks
antiPatterns: Using OrbitControls or addon imports that are not available on the r128 CDN
Using CapsuleGeometry which requires r142 or later versions
Forgetting to add lights when using lit materials like MeshStandardMaterial

faq.question: Why does this skill use Three.js r128 specifically?
faq.answer: The r128 version is the most stable CDN release compatible with artifact environments. Newer features require npm build tools.

faq.question: Can I use OrbitControls with this setup?
faq.answer: No, OrbitControls requires importing from the examples folder which is not available on CDN. This skill teaches custom camera control implementation instead.

faq.question: How do I add custom 3D models like GLTF files?
faq.answer: GLTF loading requires the GLTFLoader addon from npm. For CDN-only projects, create geometry programmatically using BufferGeometry or primitive shapes.

faq.question: Why is my 3D scene showing a black screen?
faq.answer: Common causes include: objects not added to scene, camera positioned incorrectly, missing renderer.render() calls, or using lit materials without lights.

faq.question: What is the maximum number of particles I should use?
faq.answer: Start with 1000-5000 particles for smooth 60fps performance. Test on target devices and reduce count if frame rate drops.

faq.question: How do I make my 3D objects look more realistic?
faq.answer: Use MeshStandardMaterial with proper lighting setup, enable renderer antialiasing, add shadows with shadowMap.enabled, and apply appropriate tone mapping.
